title: Close the empty-host no-auth footgun so an empty TEAMKB_API_HOST cannot bind all interfaces while passing the loopback assertion
description: Adversarial verdict gapClosed=false. TEAMKB_API_HOST set to empty or whitespace passes isLoopbackHost (returns true for '') but flows to app.listen host '' which Node binds to all interfaces. NODE_ENV unset plus no tokens plus empty host equals unauthenticated admin reachable off-host. Trim and coerce empty/whitespace host to 127.0.0.1 at config load, or make isLoopbackHost('') return false.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true: loadConfig fed TEAMKB_API_HOST empty, whitespace, and undefined all resolve config.host to 127.0.0.1 via ?.trim() || default (the || is load-bearing; ?? was the bug); isLoopbackHost('') now false so a direct empty-host bypass trips the off-loopback boot refusal for an empty token registry. config.test.ts 5 cases pass, apps/api 276/276. Shipped on draft PR jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#205 (commit 72e56e1).

title: Derive the effective tenant server-side so an omitted tenantId can never fall through to an unfiltered all-tenant query
description: Adversarial verdict EMPIRICALLY REPRODUCED a cross-tenant read leak: a team-alpha-scoped token issuing POST /api/search with tenantId omitted returned a team-beta memory (200, totalCount 1). The tenancy guard is negative-only. Make SearchQuery.tenantId server-overridden not client-optional, inject the token tenant when omitted, and fix the inert qmd-adapter mismatch guard when tenantId is undefined.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true: team-alpha token POST /api/search with tenantId OMITTED (and 6 bypass variants: array, object, empty-string, GET /api/memories, query-array, multi-tenant-400) returned 0 beta rows. Two server-side layers: tenancy-guard preHandler resolves effective tenant from request.tenants; qmd-adapter guard de-inerted to positive-match fail-closed. apps/api tenancy 23/23, qmd-adapter 7/7. Shipped on draft PR jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#205 (commit 72e56e1).

title: Scan the persisted metadata_json and author_json free-text fields at the CandidateRepository.insert choke point
description: Adversarial verdict gapClosed=false. assertDisclosureClean scans only content/title/tags; insert() persists metadata_json (projectContext, filePaths, repoUrl, branch, language, sessionId) and author_json (name,id) UNFILTERED, so an SSN in projectContext, a token in repoUrl, or PII in author.name reaches durable governed state. Extend the choke point to scan the serialized metadata_json and author_json before INSERT.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true. Root-cause structural fix: replaced the hand-maintained DisclosureScanInput subset with collectFreeTextFields(), a depth-bounded cycle-guarded walk that scans EVERY persisted string surface (content, title, tags, all metadata free-text, author.name/id, AND tenant_id - the leaked column); only an explicit ENUM_CONSTRAINED_FIELDS allow-list is skipped, inverting the failure mode to fail-safe. New schema-coverage guard test derives the persisted free-text leaf set from the live Zod schemas and fails if any leaf is unscanned (verified red on a simulated new field). Skeptic enumerated columns from the DDL+insert() itself and drove the real insert() with 50 probes (SSN/comp/token/homoglyph across every free-text column): all rejected count==0, clean control writes. common 110, store 155, apps/api 276, typecheck 0. Shipped on draft PR jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#205 (commit 9cfcbfd).

title: Harden the single-user governed brain before any teammate logs in: PII repository-layer choke point, token-bound tenancy, token hashing/expiry/revocation, ICO-to-INTKB spool wiring, and backup/DR
description: EPIC 0 of the de-risked governed-brain plan. The security review found live holes in shipped code: the PII/comp filter guards only 1 of 3+ intake doors (bulk import, MCP propose, ico ingest bypass it); tenant is client-asserted not token-bound (cross-tenant read/write plus qmd search leak); bearer tokens are plaintext with no rotation/expiry/revocation; NODE_ENV-unset silently grants admin no-auth; the ICO-to-INTKB spool handoff is not wired in prod. Fix all as a repository-layer choke point plus token-derived tenancy plus hashed/expirable/revocable tokens plus wired spool plus restore-tested backup/DR. Gates teammate onboarding via the shared-server model (not distributed clones).
notes:
EPIC 0 hardening landed as draft PR jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#205 (one squashed clean commit; all contiguous secret-shaped test fixtures fragmented repo-wide except the allowlisted AWS example; touched suites green; push-protection passed). Three residuals remain OPEN and gate teammate onboarding: c5k.1 metadata/author scan, c5k.2 omitted-tenantId search leak, c5k.3 empty-host footgun.
EPIC 0 hardening MERGED to qmd-team-intent-kb main via PR #205 (squash). Delivered: PII repository-layer choke point with a schema-derived scan set + coverage guard, token-bound tenancy, token hashing/expiry/revocation, spool wiring. c5k.1/.2/.3 all closed adversarially. Remaining under c5k: c5k.4 backup/DR (blocked on R2 creds) and c5k.5 enum defense-in-depth (LOW).

title: Deploy the single remote governed brain (INTKB Fastify API) on the tailnet team-server and seed tokens.json
description: WORK REPO: qmd-team-intent-kb/apps/api. The Fastify API (apps/api/src/main.ts; TEAMKB_API_HOST/PORT, TEAMKB_TOKENS_FILE, scrypt-hashed token registry) is BUILT but not deployed. Stand it up on the tailnet team-server (D27 says start there, not the VPS), bind the tailnet IP, seed tokens.json with per-user scrypt-hashed bearer tokens. No Dockerfile exists yet — add deploy config. Depends-on: compile-then-govern-c5k (multi-user hardening — already shipped via #205; c5k.4 backup/DR still open/blocked on R2 creds). Gates U2 (team-mode wiring) and D2 team-mode dogfood.
notes: Depends-on: compile-then-govern-c5k (multi-user hardening) — ALREADY SHIPPED via PR #205 (PII repo-layer choke, token-bound tenancy, scrypt token hashing/expiry/revocation, spool wiring). Only c5k.4 (backup/DR, blocked on R2 creds) remains before a fully production-grade deploy; a soak/dogfood deploy can proceed now.

title: Wire the unified plugin's team mode to the D27 single remote brain over the tailnet, reusing the existing remote client
description: WORK REPO: governed-second-brain-plugin. After U1 lands the dispatcher and after the team-server is deployed, point team mode at the live D27 remote brain (TEAMKB_API_URL + per-user token), confirm brain_search proxies and returns the same qmd:// cited shape over the proxy. Depends-on: compile-then-govern-650 (U1), the deploy bead, and the Phase-0 guards (cze.1, 3iu).

title: Merge governed-second-brain (local) and intent-brain (team) into one plugin: mode dispatcher on TEAMKB_API_URL, bundle both servers, rename teamkb_* to brain_*
description: WORK REPO: governed-second-brain-plugin. Repo 1 (governed-second-brain v0.1.7) is the unified home. Add src/index.ts dispatcher that starts the in-process local-server (src/local-server.ts, 6 brain_* tools) when TEAMKB_API_URL is unset, else the remote client (moved from qmd-team-intent-kb/apps/mcp-server/src/remote-client.ts -> src/remote-server.ts). Bundle both via build.mjs. Rename the remote tool teamkb_search -> brain_search. One .mcp.json server entry exposing both env schemas (TEAMKB_TENANT_ID local; TEAMKB_API_URL + TEAMKB_API_TOKEN team). Bump plugin.json to v1.0.0. Update skills/brain + skills/brain-save wording for both modes. Verify with smoke.mjs (local) + a team-mode smoke against a stub. No dep — can start now (parallel to guards).
notes: CI on PR #6 GREEN: Full-chain smoke (zero egress) passed (2m36s), CodeRabbit pass. Ready to merge.
close_reason: U1 merged: governed-second-brain-plugin#6 (squash). Unified plugin v1.0.0 with local|team mode dispatcher; both-mode smokes + full-chain CI green.

title: Unify the two governed-brain plugins into one local|team-mode product; retire the standalone intent-brain plugin
description:
The public governed-second-brain plugin (local, in-process ~/.teamkb) and the private intent-brain plugin (remote tailnet proxy) are almost the same product — they already link the same @qmd-team-intent-kb governance packages; the only real difference is transport + write-path. Unify into ONE plugin with two runtime modes dispatched by TEAMKB_API_URL: local mode (default, in-process, public showcase) and team mode (remote proxy to the D27 single remote brain). One MCP entry, one tool surface (brain_*). Only the DATA + TEAMKB_API_URL + token stay private. Aligns ratified decision D27 (single remote brain, build-not-decide). Supersedes the intent-brain install bug (kot).

RECONCILED GROUND TRUTH (2026-06-20): the multi-user hardening (c5k) and the INTKB candidate-intake disclosure scan are ALREADY SHIPPED via PR #205 — a repository-layer choke (assertDisclosureClean at CandidateRepository.insert) covering comp/PII/secrets across all free-text fields, token-bound tenancy, token hashing/expiry/revocation, spool wiring. Remaining true gaps: cze.1 (ICO ingest-side disclosure guard — ICO writes its own workspace/DB before spooling and has NO comp/PII scan today), and 3iu's one-shot propose->promote. Deploy still needed (D27 team-server).

title: Merge the intent-brain marketplace source-object-form fix to unblock installing the plugin on stable Claude Code
description: Draft PR intent-solutions-io/claude-plugins#1 converts marketplace.json source from a bare string to object form so stable Claude Code 2.1.183 accepts it, unblocking Ope. Closes bead compile-then-govern-kot. No CI on the catalog repo; review and merge.
close_reason: Duplicate of the canonical bead compile-then-govern-kot (the intent-brain marketplace fix). Draft PR intent-solutions-io/claude-plugins#1 addresses it; tracking stays on kot.

title: Rotate the four exposed dhat.v1 DoltHub tokens pasted raw into the Claude history file and store the new token only in SOPS
description: Jeremy manual action. Four distinct dhat.v1 token values are live and unrotated in ~/.claude/history.jsonl (lines around 32122, 32125, 32235/32916, 44386). Anyone with read access can push to DoltHub as jeremylongshore. Rotate at dolthub account settings, store new token only in SOPS, never paste into chat.

title: Carry-forward cluster: rotate exposed DoltHub tokens, merge the intent-brain marketplace fix, and fix the dead ICO spool config path
description: Loose ends surfaced by the EPIC 0 workflow that are not part of the hardening branch itself.

title: Build the govern-at-merge gate as a post-merge re-derivation pass over the union that re-governs every merged row as untrusted
description: The decisive spike evidence: dolt merge admitted a secret row with zero governance. Build a post-merge re-derivation pass (dedupe, secret, disclosure, length with an accreting existingHashes set), proven commutative (A union B equals B union A byte-identical), that re-governs every merged row as untrusted. This is the layer Dolt structurally lacks.
close_reason: Govern-at-merge gate shipped + adversarially proven. Draft PR #207 (qmd-team-intent-kb, branch feat/epic1-govern-at-merge-gate, commit ca34659, NOT merged). mergeGovern() is a re-derivation-over-union pass reusing every existing primitive: union + id-dedup by content-derived UUIDv5, sort-by-id (the one new discipline = commutativity keystone), re-project each row to untrusted MemoryCandidate, assertDisclosureClean() fail-closed first, then full PolicyPipeline with accreting existingHashes seeded from getAllContentHashes(), promote survivors via canonical path with deterministic mergeClock so timestamps + audit chain re-walk identically across clones. Independent auditor (not reusing curator fixtures, called public mergeGovern entrypoint) confirmed gapClosed=true: (a) AWS-key/SSN/salary content rows AND a metadata-buried AWS key all QUARANTINED at the disclosure choke point, absent from durable curated_memories; (b) cross-clone content duplicate deduped to exactly one survivor by content-id; (c) A-union-B vs B-union-A byte-identical governed state (sha256 4ae3c0ee...) AND identical audit chain (sha256 28f0608a...), both verifyAuditChain breaks=0, byte-identity held even when the same union is partitioned across clones differently; (d) every survivor independently re-passed assertDisclosureClean + PolicyPipeline. Differential mutation testing proved tests non-vacuous (bypassing disclosure gate -> secrets rode in; admitting policy-rejected rows -> dedup probe failed), then source reverted byte-identical (sha256 match vs HEAD). Three smuggle vectors all FAILED (metadata free-text secret, content-id collision, reordering attack). 602 tests green; CI all-green (validate/gitleaks/semgrep). Working tree clean, on feature branch. EPIC 1 children 8da.7 (Ed25519 signed DAG anchor) + 8da.8 (merge-aware verifier owning canonical ordering) now build on this.

title: Build a merge-aware audit verifier that validates each clone chain plus the re-derived merged chain plus a signed DAG anchor
description: Build the merge-aware audit verifier: validate each clone chain in isolation, the re-derived merged chain, and a signed DAG anchor listing both parent heads. Dispatch on hash_version (v1 timestamp-in-hash vs v2 timestamp-excluded; the per-row dispatch already in audit-verify.ts is the template). OWNS cross-clone ORDERING reconciliation: define a canonical event ordering (by content-derived event id) so two clones that promoted the same set in different order reconcile to the same verified state - this resolves the ordering residual surfaced on 8da.6. The current linear verifier breaks at a merge boundary with PREV_LINK_MISMATCH.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true. Merge-aware verifier verifyMergeAuditChain: per-clone linear validation (ENTRY_HASH_MISMATCH on a tampered clone), verifier-owned canonical content-id ordering (canonicalMergeOrder, not gate-trusted), merged re-walk (PREV_LINK on a reordered merge), and signed DAG anchor cross-check (DAG_SIGNATURE_INVALID / DAG_PARENT_MISMATCH / DAG_HEAD_MISMATCH / DAG_ANCHOR_MISSING). 180/180 store tests, lint/typecheck clean. PR #208 commit 40fc800.

title: Wire the dead audit-anchor.ts into a per-actor Ed25519 signed chain-head with DAG-parent fields and a Lamport logical clock
description: audit-anchor.ts already exists but has zero production callers (the dead code from the tokens-auth finding). Wire it so the anchor binds chainHead plus commitHash plus logical clock plus parents[], signed per actor with native Node Ed25519, so cross-clone non-repudiation is real and not documentation-only.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true by an independent harness (real production modules, not the impl's own tests). Per-actor Ed25519 signed DAG merge anchor: parents[]=the two clone heads as an order-independent set, lamportClock, embedded signer public key, detached Ed25519 signature over the canonical merged head. The bare hash-chain was defeated by tamper-then-rehash (verifyAuditChain breaks:0) but the signed anchor CAUGHT it (HISTORY_REWRITTEN/DAG_HEAD_MISMATCH); a keyless forger failed (20,000 brute signatures, 0 accepted). No private key committed (ephemeral keys in tests, .gitignore blocks plaintext). 180/180 store tests. PR #208 commit c8de18e.

title: Make audit-chain timestamps deterministic so two clones promoting the same logical event produce identical entry_hash
description: The single most load-bearing EPIC 1 change. audit-chain.ts hashes the timestamp into entry_hash, so wallclock new Date() makes two clones produce different hashes for the same logical event. Derive the canonical-row timestamp from candidate.capturedAt or exclude timestamp from the canonical hash. Requires a canonical-row rehash migration, not a drop-in.
notes: Design call (steering): the v2 entry_hash is intentionally chain-position-dependent (it folds in prev_entry_hash) - correct for a hash chain. The foundation invariant is therefore 'same ordered event set -> same chain head; same logical event with a deterministic id at the same position -> same entry_hash', NOT per-event order-independence. Cross-clone reconciliation of differently-ordered chains is delegated to the merge-aware verifier 8da.8. Remaining blocker before close: the random audit-event id (promoter.ts randomUUID at ~137/156/179/198/220 is hashed into entry_hash) is being made content-derived; re-verify the corrected invariant then close 8da.5+8da.6.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true (corrected invariant: same ordered set -> same chain head; per-event order-independence is by-design, delegated to 8da.8). v2 canonical serialiser excludes wallclock (timestamp-independent entry_hash proven across 2026/2027/2029/2030); non-destructive migration keeps v1 frozen tamper-evidence; mixed v1/v2 verifies in one pass via per-row hash_version dispatch. Combined with the deterministic audit-event id fix, the real promote() path reproduces byte-identical entry_hash across clones. INTKB #206 commit 55cff98.

title: Replace random UUID v4 with content-derived UUID v5 in ICO sources.ts and INTKB promoter.ts for cross-clone reproducibility
description: memoryId derived from candidateId plus contentHash so the same logical memory gets the same id across clones. Replicate the existing uuidV5 helper in ICO spool.ts. Trace the full id lineage spool to import to promoter so the random v4 in import-pipeline.ts does not break the chain. Foundation primitive; land first.
close_reason: Adversarially verified gapClosed=true. Content-derived UUID v5 is byte-identical cross-clone AND cross-repo (golden vector e0e430cb...). Audit-event ids + link ids also content-derived (deriveAuditEventId/deriveLinkId), so the real promote() path is deterministic; no randomUUID reachable on the hashed path (policyId stays random but is never hashed, verified). Real two-clone reproduction at wallclocks years apart: candidateId/memoryId/auditEventId/entry_hash/chainHead all byte-identical. ICO #143 (shared module) + INTKB #206 commit 55cff98. store 161, curator 243, common 125, typecheck 0.

title: Ship a quiesced restore-tested backup and DR path for the teamkb.db brain store with an off-host encrypted copy
description: Recon-only today, nothing shipped; borg covers a live WAL hot-copy, not a restore-tested quiesced backup. Wire VACUUM INTO (safe with the live teamkb-brain-api writer) plus PRAGMA integrity_check plus age-encrypt to the existing recipient plus rclone copy to a Cloudflare R2 bucket, as a systemd user timer after borg-backup.timer, with a download/decrypt/row-count restore round-trip as the acceptance gate. Blocked on R2 credential provisioning (Jeremy manual action).

title: Fix intent-brain plugin source in intent-solutions-io/claude-plugins marketplace so stable Claude Code can install it
description: Ope (CTO) is blocked installing intent-brain@intent-solutions-io on stable Claude Code 2.1.183: error 'This plugin uses a source type your Claude Code version does not support.' Cause: the intent-brain entry in the intent-solutions-io/claude-plugins marketplace uses a bare-string source (jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb); stable Claude Code requires the object source form (source github + repo jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb) for external-repo plugin sources. Also: qmd-team-intent-kb needs a root .claude-plugin/plugin.json (or strict:false + a skills array in the marketplace entry) or install fails at the next step. Ope offered to PR the one-liner.
notes: Superseded-by: compile-then-govern-650 (the plugin unification). The unification absorbs intent-brain as the unified plugin's team mode, which fixes this install bug at the root rather than patching the bare-string marketplace source. Will be formally closed when 650.3 (publish) lands.

title: Anchor the audit chain head externally via git-exporter or OpenTimestamps before any cross-actor tamper-evident claim ships
notes: Substrate DONE + green: external audit-anchor primitive added to INTKB store — appendAnchor (append-only, hash-chained anchor log of the chain head) + verifyAnchors (cross-checks live chain vs anchored snapshots). Catches HISTORY_REWRITTEN — the silent full re-hash-forward rewrite that verifyAuditChain passes clean. PR jeremylongshore/qmd-team-intent-kb#187 (5/5 tests, validate green). REMAINING to apply in the product: (1) merge #187; (2) rebuild the plugin bundle with the updated store; (3) wire govern.ts to appendAnchor after each govern + git-commit/push the anchor file (the external tamper-evidence); (4) add a verify path; (5) update the umbrella README 'what the receipt does NOT do' box — the anchor now EXISTS (no longer 'on the roadmap').
close_reason: DONE end-to-end: anchor primitive merged to INTKB (#187); plugin govern.ts commits the chain head to a git-backed append-only anchor log per pass; brain_audit_verify (verifyAnchors) detects chain breaks AND silent full rewrites; README honesty box updated (implemented, not roadmap); republished governed-second-brain@0.1.3 with npm provenance. Verified: govern anchored{committed:true}+git commit, verify clean on intact brain, tamper→ok=false (ANCHOR_HASH_MISMATCH+HISTORY_REWRITTEN).

title: Gate Phase A green with validate-skillmd marketplace tier and validate-plugin marketplace tier
close_reason: validate-plugin green: manifest canonical, .mcp.json kebab+stdio+no-network, both skills 8-field marketplace tier Grade B (no errors), allowed-tools↔bundle alignment confirmed (5/5).

title: Author the public plugin.json, local .mcp.json, and marketplace.json for Governed Second Brain
close_reason: Authored .claude-plugin/plugin.json (Apache-2.0, commands:[], homepage==repository), local .mcp.json (governed-brain stdio, TEAMKB_TENANT_ID=local, no API_URL), and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (category productivity). All validate.

title: Reframe the brain and brain-save skills for public single-user use and align allowed-tools with the real MCP tool surface
close_reason: Wrote public skills/{brain,brain-save}/SKILL.md — scrubbed team/admin/tailnet/403; single-user framing; allowed-tools (mcp__governed-brain__brain_*) exactly match the 5 registered tools. Dead-tool gap closed; both skills marketplace Grade B.

title: Build the public local stdio MCP server that drives qmd, the govern kernel, and ~/.teamkb in-process, exposing read (search) and write (capture, govern, promote)
close_reason: Built src/local-server.ts + src/govern.ts: self-contained in-process stdio MCP server (brain_search/status read; brain_capture/govern/transition write). brain_govern hand-wires ingest→Curator.processBatch→runExport→qmd index, daemon-free. Smoke-proven end-to-end (751bbe5).

title: Add a permanent same-content/different-id regression test to merge-gate.test.ts so the policy content-hash dedup cannot be silently shadowed
description: The merge-gate's policy content-hash dedup_check is shadowed by the upstream content-derived-id dedup in the common path, so the committed merge-gate.test.ts does not exercise a same-content/different-id row; the 8da.9 auditor surfaced the policy-bypass only with a hand-crafted probe. Add a permanent same-content/different-id regression test to the committed suite so a future regression disabling the pipeline-verdict branch fails CI instead of passing on the disclosure layer alone.

title: Retire the standalone intent-brain plugin listing now that it is the unified plugin's team mode
description: WORK REPO: qmd-team-intent-kb (.claude-plugin) + catalog. Remove/redirect the standalone intent-brain marketplace listing; it is absorbed as the unified plugin's team mode. Depends-on: U3 (publish).

title: Publish the unified plugin to the public marketplace (local) and the private intent-solutions-io/claude-plugins (team); supersede the intent-brain install bug
description: WORK REPO: governed-second-brain-plugin + intent-solutions-io/claude-plugins. Ship the unified v1.0.0 plugin to the public marketplace (local mode showcase) and the private intent-solutions-io/claude-plugins catalog (team mode). This SUPERSEDES compile-then-govern-kot (the intent-brain bare-string source install bug) — the unification is the fix. Depends-on: U2.

title: Guard the locked UUID-v5 namespace against cross-repo drift (vendored byte-identical in ICO and INTKB)
description: The content-derived id depends on a locked namespace UUID (6c6f6e67-7368-6f72-6500-69636f73706c) now vendored in two repos (ICO types and INTKB packages/common/src/uuid-v5.ts). If it drifts, dedupe breaks and the audit-chain candidateId foreign key breaks silently. Add a CI cross-repo constant-equality check (or extract to one shared package) plus a golden-vector test pinning e0e430cb-ede6-53ae-8bd0-1edc3b945c6f so any drift fails loudly. Consider a second-Node-runtime check to close JSON.stringify key-order portability doubt. Surfaced by the EPIC 1 foundation adversarial verify.

title: Re-assert enum-field membership at the repository layer so a raw insert caller cannot smuggle disclosure content through a closed-vocab field
description: Defense-in-depth follow-up from the c5k.1 adversarial probe. collectFreeTextFields() skips ENUM_CONSTRAINED_FIELDS (status, source, category, trustLevel, confidence, sensitivity, type) by name, so a RAW caller planting an SSN-shaped value under category/status/source/author.type bypasses the disclosure scan and insert() writes it raw (count==1). UNREACHABLE in production (apps/curator import-pipeline and spool-intake hardcode these from closed vocab), but a future raw caller forwarding untrusted enum values without a Zod parse would re-open it. Fix: re-assert MemoryCandidate.parse() or enum membership at the repo layer, or scan enum fields that FAIL their closed-vocab check. LOW severity, not blocking PR #205.

title: Refresh the ecosystem system-map and dependency diagram to show the Dolt-substrate plus govern-at-merge two-layer model
description: DEFERRED until the spike primitives land.

title: Write the EPIC 1 govern-at-merge decision record (AT-DECR) capturing the GO verdict and the Dolt-native-merge-admits-secret evidence
description: DEFERRED. Capture the GO verdict, the dolt merge admits a secret row evidence, the adopt-internally-plus-contribute-upstream call, and the four bounded caveats.

title: DEFERRED (demand-gated): record the EPIC 1 govern-at-merge decision and refresh the ecosystem system-map
description: DEFERRED behind the demand gate. Build only after EPIC 0 residuals and EPIC 1 primitives are green.

title: Fix the dead ICO CLI spool.tenantId config path so the advertised config is read instead of silently requiring --tenant
description: loadConfig has no .ico/config.json or spool key support, so the advertised spool.tenantId config path does not work and operators must pass --tenant. Either implement the config read or correct the error message. Surfaced by the ICO spool PR #142.

title: Prototype the govern merge-gate: two clones, a promote on each, a pull that runs the policy pipeline at the boundary
description: Two Dolt clones of one brain; promote a memory on each; pull one into the other and run dedupe -> policy -> promotion AT MERGE. Decide whether conflict resolution between two promoted memories is deterministic. This is the crux of the distributed-remote model (000-docs/001-RR-DOLT section 5/7).
close_reason: Resolved by the EPIC 1 spike: built a real Dolt DB, branched into two clones, promoted independently on each, ran dolt merge (admitted a secret row with zero governance), then ran a post-merge re-derivation pass that quarantined the secret and re-committed governed state. That is exactly this bead's prototype. Artifacts in /tmp: spike-governed-merge.mjs (18/18 pass) + dolt-merge-probe/.

title: Spike the brain's durable state on a Dolt database and port audit-verify to read the commit graph
description: Throwaway branch: model INTKB's durable store on a Dolt DB; port audit-verify to read the Dolt commit graph instead of the hand-rolled JSONL hash-chain; confirm 'ico audit verify' semantics still hold. Evidence in, decision later.

title: Evaluate Dolt as the governed-second-brain substrate and scope a possible DoltHub collaboration
description: Assess whether Dolt (the version-controlled SQL DB the stack already rides via Beads) should back the brain's own durable state, and whether the 'distributed-remote' model (clone, pull, merge; govern-at-merge; audit = Dolt commit graph) is the honest answer to multiplayer/teammate sharing. Captured in 000-docs/001-RR-DOLT. Treat as a spike, not a migration — nothing ships until evidence earns it. Relationship/outreach context is in private auto-memory (dolt-collaboration-grounding), deliberately NOT in beads since this DB syncs to a public remote.
notes:
GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#8 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/8. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-8da` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-8da` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

Plane: BRAIN-7 — https://projects.intentsolutions.io/internal/projects/?peekIssue=BRAIN-7

title: Upgrade brain_search to qmd hybrid query (auto-expansion + rerank) plus an embed step in govern, for natural-language recall
description: brain_search currently runs qmd search (BM25, AND-semantics) which is brittle for natural-language queries: hyphenated/compound terms (e.g. 'in-process') tokenize to zero matches and fail the AND, so verbose questions often return nothing. qmd query (hybrid: auto-expansion + vector + LLM rerank) handles NL far better but needs qmd embed (a one-time ~333MB model download + embedding pass). Decide whether to add an embed step to brain_govern and switch brain_search to qmd query. Shared improvement (the internal brain uses the same BM25 adapter).
notes: Empirical confirmation (this session): qmd hybrid 'query' with the full NL question 'does the brain run in-process without a daemon' → 1 hit, while BM25 'search in-process' → 0. The upgrade path (switch brain_search to qmd query + add an embed step in govern) fixes the natural-language recall gap. Cost: ~333MB embedding model + one embed pass per govern.
close_reason: Folded into the qmd-team-intent-kb-0t9 native-retrieval-backend epic per user decision 2026-06-17 ('defer into 0t9'). brain_search semantic recall will land via 0t9's on-device sqlite-vec/hybrid backend, NOT a standalone qmd-hybrid + ~333MB-qmd-embedding-model path the native backend would supersede (avoids throwaway work; keeps the plugin's footprint light). Current BM25 cited (qmd://) search stays as-is until then. Scope addendum recorded on qmd-team-intent-kb-0t9.

title: Add gsb.lock.json reproducible pin and a CI smoke that runs the full chain against the pinned set
close_reason: gsb.lock.json pins the known-good tuple: plugin 0.1.4 x ICO 1.14.0 x INTKB 0.6.0@f800797 (bundled) x qmd 2.0.1 (@tobilu/qmd) + node>=20/better-sqlite3 floor. smoke/smoke.mjs drives the committed MCP bundle through capture->govern->audit-verify->search over a real stdio session, isolated temp TEAMKB_BASE_PATH (never touches a live ~/.teamkb), hermetic + zero LLM egress. check-lock.mjs is a drift guard (gsb.lock/plugin.json/marketplace.json/.mcp.json all == package.json version) — turns the version drift validate-plugin caught into a hard CI fail. .github/workflows/smoke.yml green on a clean ubuntu runner incl. the best-effort qmd:// cited hit (run 27668628775).

title: Harden installer security: npm provenance and checksums, pin qmd and ICO by SHA or tag, mint a local token, drop curl-bash
notes: Basic npm publish DONE: governed-second-brain@0.1.1 live (npx governed-second-brain init verified from the registry end-to-end). Caught+fixed a packaging bug (0.1.0 baked a linux-only better-sqlite3 via a files glob → 4.2MB; unpublished; 0.1.1 ships only the .cjs, 249KB, native dep installs per-platform). Installer pins ICO @^1.14.0. REMAINING for this bead: npm provenance (needs a CI release workflow w/ OIDC, like ICO's release.yml), 2FA/OTP on publish, checksums, and the local-token mint.
close_reason: Installer/publish hardening done: (1) npm PROVENANCE via a CI release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml, OIDC id-token, tag-version guard) — governed-second-brain@0.1.2 carries a SLSA provenance attestation + registry signature; (2) ICO pinned @^1.14.0; (3) qmd version-check (>=2.x); (4) runtime fully bundled (ajv inlined, only better-sqlite3 external); (5) install is npx, not curl|bash; (6) loud pre-flight consent. N/A for the single-trust-domain local stdio model: local-token mint + origin/host-header check (those are for the future hosted tier). Reproducible pin (gsb.lock.json) tracked separately in qy7.10.

title: Build the one-command npx installer that builds the local brain over a folder, idempotent with --resume and --clean
notes:
Phase B milestone (e1b5104, plugin repo): bin/init.mjs 'init <folder> --index-only' works end-to-end — consent pre-flight, vendors better-sqlite3, drives the bundled MCP server to capture each file → govern → qmd index. Verified: 3 captured → 3 promoted, searchable with qmd:// citations, audit chain verifies (3 events, 0 breaks), ZERO egress. --clean + additive-default idempotency. Remaining for this bead/Phase B: full ICO-compile mode (opt-in egress, qy7.6), automatic Claude Code/Cowork MCP registration, and npx publish + provenance (qy7.9).
Added automatic Claude Code MCP registration (e663889): installer runs 'claude mcp add governed-brain' (idempotent, --scope user default, --no-register opt-out) pointing at the vendored runtime + brain env; falls back to printing the command if claude CLI absent. Verified at project scope (writes correct .mcp.json, claude mcp list sees it, global config untouched). So 'init --index-only' is now a true one-command experience for the local/no-egress path: build brain → register → use. Remaining: full ICO-compile mode (egress), Cowork registration, npx publish+provenance (qy7.9).
Full ICO-compile mode wired + verified (plugin 705e686). 'init <folder>' (no --index-only) runs ico init→ingest→compile all→spool→brain_govern; uses DeepSeek (ICO_PROVIDER=deepseek), NOT Anthropic credits. Verified: 2-file corpus → 6 passes → 10 derived memories promoted → searchable + audited (11 events, 0 breaks). Installer now covers: index-only (zero-egress), full-compile (DeepSeek), native-dep vendoring, auto Claude Code MCP registration. Remaining: npx publish+provenance (qy7.9), Cowork registration.
close_reason: npx installer complete + published (governed-second-brain@0.1.6, SLSA-provenanced). bin/init.mjs: 'npx governed-second-brain init <folder>' builds the local brain end-to-end — pre-flight consent (--yes skips), resolveIco (sibling checkout → ico on PATH → npx -y intentional-cognition-os@^1.14.0 pinned), then either --index-only (ZERO LLM egress: qmd-only index) OR full ICO compile (DeepSeek provider via ICO_PROVIDER=deepseek, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY required) → INTKB govern → qmd index. ensureNativeDep builds better-sqlite3; registerMcp via 'claude mcp add' (--no-register skips, --scope sets user/project). Idempotent + resumable: additive by default so --resume is a no-op, ensureNativeDep skips when the addon already exists; --clean wipes the target brain dir + rebuilds. --base/--tenant give isolated brains (the CI smoke uses an isolated TEAMKB_BASE_PATH, never a live ~/.teamkb). End-to-end verified by .github/workflows/smoke.yml (capture→govern→audit-verify→search green on a clean runner).

title: Decide and implement the installer fetch model: publish the needed INTKB packages to npm versus ship a prebuilt bundle
close_reason: Resolved: ship the prebuilt esbuild bundle that inlines the 8 INTKB workspace packages (INTKB never published, stays private). ICO consumed via npx intentional-cognition-os@^1.14.0; only better-sqlite3 installs per-platform. Plugin published as governed-second-brain on npm.

title: Add an index-only no-egress mode and a loud pre-flight egress consent for the ICO compile step
close_reason: Both done: --index-only no-egress mode (capture→govern→index, zero network) + a loud pre-flight egress consent on the full-compile path. Verified end-to-end.

title: Productize Governed Second Brain into a public local-first plugin, installer, and marketplace listing
description: Umbrella epic for turning the Compile-Then-Govern stack into a public, local-first Governed Second Brain that outsiders (esp. the Cowork community) can install and build their own governed, qmd://-cited brain with a tamper-evident audit trail, from inside Claude Code or Cowork. Internal brain + team mode stay untouched. Phases: A public local-MCP plugin (read+write) · B npx installer (egress consent + --index-only) · C marketplace distribution · D cross-link/thesis-fold/tracking. Plus standalone MUST-FIX beads (audit-anchor HIGH, egress mode, installer security, gsb.lock.json).
notes:
GitHub: intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain#1 — https://github.com/intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain/issues/1. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note compile-then-govern-qy7` and `bd-sync close compile-then-govern-qy7` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
Phase A landed (751bbe5): the public local-first MCP plugin is built and gate-green. Read+write tools (brain_search/status, brain_capture/govern/transition) run in-process, daemon-free; capture→govern→promote→search verified end-to-end with a qmd:// citation and a verified SHA-256 hash-chained audit (1 event, 0 breaks). Both skills pass marketplace 8-field tier (Grade B); the original dead-tool gap is closed (allowed-tools match the 5 registered tools). Beads qy7.1–qy7.4 closed. Known follow-up filed: natural-language retrieval (qmd hybrid query + embed). Next: Phase B installer.
Repo restructure (per ownership decision): the public PLUGIN moved to a new personal repo jeremylongshore/governed-second-brain-plugin (smoke-green in its new home). intent-solutions-io/governed-second-brain reverts to umbrella/landing-only and points at the plugin + engines. Engines (ICO, INTKB) stay personal. Company org = umbrella + private internal catalog. Program beads + this issue stay on the umbrella as the program tracker. Phase B installer will live in the plugin repo.
DeepSeek wired into ICO (engine) to power full-compile without Anthropic credits — PR jeremylongshore/intentional-cognition-os#137 MERGED (provider switch ICO_PROVIDER=deepseek, default anthropic unchanged; default path verified byte-identical, 31 regression tests + CI green). Installer full-compile now runs on DeepSeek end-to-end against local ICO. Internal team compile UNAFFECTED (additive, opt-in; the intent-brain plugin doesn't run compile).
Distribution live: ICO auto-released v1.14.0 with DeepSeek (npx intentional-cognition-os@1.14.0); plugin published governed-second-brain@0.1.1 — 'npx governed-second-brain init <folder>' works end-to-end from npm (both --index-only and full-compile-on-DeepSeek). qy7.7 closed (bundle, not publish-INTKB). qy7.9 remaining: provenance/2FA/checksums.
Security hardening landed: governed-second-brain@0.1.2 published with npm PROVENANCE (SLSA attestation via CI OIDC release workflow). Also verified this turn: INTKB repo builds clean (only the flagged DECR license edit in its tree); the live ~/.teamkb brain is untouched (680 memories, last modified Jun 14). qy7.7 + qy7.9 closed. Remaining: gsb.lock.json reproducible pin + CI full-chain smoke (qy7.10), Cowork registration, audit-anchor HIGH (qy7.5), cross-link/docs (qy7.12).
close_reason: Governed Second Brain productization COMPLETE — all 13 children closed. Shipped: (A) public Claude Code + Cowork plugin — local in-process stdio MCP, read+write (brain_search/status/audit_verify + capture/govern/transition), governance-on, scrubbed of internal refs, marketplace-tier validate-plugin gate green; (B) npx installer 'governed-second-brain init <folder>' — pinned ICO+qmd, index-only zero-egress mode, DeepSeek-compile mode (no Anthropic credits), auto MCP registration, native-dep build, idempotent/--clean/--resume; (C) audit anchor — external append-only anchor log + git commit closes the silent-rewrite gap verifyAuditChain missed (brain_audit_verify cross-checks chain + anchors); (D) honesty fixes — tamper-DETECTION wording, no-egress mode, license/version consistency across all repos/badges/DECRs; (E) reproducibility — gsb.lock.json known-good tuple + hermetic full-chain CI smoke + drift guard; (F) distribution — self-host marketplace.json + big-marketplace sources.yaml (functional via inline plugin.json mcpServers); (G) hardening — DB tools degrade actionably on file-copy installs. Released governed-second-brain@0.1.6 (SLSA-provenanced). qy7.13 (semantic recall) folded into the qmd-team-intent-kb-0t9 native-backend epic per user decision. Internal ~/.teamkb brain + intent-brain plugin untouched throughout.

title: Exercise N-way merge associativity and commutativity (fold of 3+ clones) before relying on multi-clone merges in production
description: mergeGovern is 2-arg; N-way merge is via fold. Associativity/commutativity over more than 2 clones was not exercised by 8da.9 verification. Add a fold-of-3 proof (byte-identical governed state + audit regardless of fold order) before multi-clone production use.

title: Assert at merge-gate entry that input memory ids are content-derived so cross-clone dedup cannot silently degrade
description: mergeGovern trusts input row .id values are content-derived (deriveMemoryId). If an upstream path mints a random v4 id for a logical memory, cross-clone dedup degrades to no-dedup (extra-row churn, not a leak; content-hash dedup still catches it under a dedup-enabled policy). Add a gate-entry invariant that input ids are deterministically derivable, failing loud otherwise.

title: Scope the AGP agent-access HTTP and mTLS surface once an external agent needs governed access
description: DEFERRED.

title: DEFERRED (demand-gated): scope AGP as an agent-access HTTP and mTLS surface in front of the governed brain
description: DEFERRED behind the demand gate. AGP is a net-new HTTP/mTLS transport plus auth build, not a version bump. Build only when an external agent actually needs governed read/write access.

title: Generalize the governed brain to distributed clones with govern-at-merge in production once demand is proven
description: DEFERRED. No speculative ecosystem-scale build.

title: DEFERRED (demand-gated): generalize the hardened single-user brain to the full multiplayer ecosystem
description: DEFERRED behind the demand gate. Distributed clones, govern-at-merge in production, audit as the Dolt commit graph. Builds only when EPIC 0 residuals plus EPIC 1 primitives are green AND real multiplayer demand is proven.

title: Draft the Dolt collaboration outreach for Jeremy to review and send
description: Prepare the re-engagement note for the open DoltHub thread. Lead with the roadmap-center hook (governance layer on Beads-on-Dolt), drop the onchain framing. Relationship/strategy context is in private auto-memory (dolt-collaboration-grounding) — NOT restated here because this DB syncs to a public remote. Jeremy authors and sends; do not send on his behalf.

title: Decide adopt-internally versus contribute-upstream for the governance-at-merge pattern, with spike evidence
description: Two independent moves: (a) use Dolt as the brain's internal store; (b) contribute the govern-at-merge pattern upstream, since it is the layer Dolt's 'agents need version control' thesis does not itself provide (history vs dedupe/policy/promotion/receipts). Gated on the two spikes (8da.1, 8da.2).

title: Cross-link and fold the compile-then-govern thesis into the umbrella and finish license and version consistency across repos, badges, and sources.yaml
close_reason: License + version + cross-link consistency landed. License floor was already uniformly Apache-2.0 (LICENSE files, package.json, README badges); the last contradicting signal was DECR 035 frontmatter (license: MIT) in ICO+INTKB — settled to Apache-2.0 with a marked editorial note preserving the dated GC 'MIT on both projects' quote (ICO #138, INTKB #188, both merged; doc byte-identical across repos). Version drift fixed: umbrella status table ICO v1.12.0->v1.14.0 + added the installable plugin row (v0.1.4, npm+SLSA); gsb.lock + CI smoke qmd 2.0.1->canonical 2.5.3 (matches INTKB's @tobilu/qmd pin), smoke green incl. cited hit. Umbrella repo rename compile-then-govern->governed-second-brain canonicalized in ICO+INTKB READMEs (internal skills left redirect-safe per 'internal stays as-is'). Umbrella already cross-links the plugin (npx governed-second-brain init). Folded prettier-normalize of ICO CLAUDE.md to clear pre-existing Format Check drift.

title: Distribute via self-host marketplace.json and a sources.yaml entry in the big marketplace, targeting a discoverable category
close_reason: Distribution complete. (1) Self-host: the plugin ships its own .claude-plugin/marketplace.json — '/plugin marketplace add jeremylongshore/governed-second-brain-plugin' installs the full functional plugin. (2) Big marketplace: sources.yaml entry merged to claude-code-plugins-plus-skills main (#873, admin-merged — all 10 required checks green; non-required link-check is historically red; also cleared a pre-existing MD012 markdownlint blocker), target plugins/mcp/governed-second-brain (the MCP-plugin convention), topical category productivity. Found + fixed a real functional gap: the external sync drops the root .mcp.json (sparse-checkout materializes it + matchesPattern keeps it, yet the git tree omits it — a sync-tooling quirk), so the synced copy registered no MCP server. Fixed by declaring the governed-brain stdio server inline under plugin.json mcpServers (released 0.1.6); the sync copies plugin.json verbatim and the marketplace's validate-mcp-config accepts mcpServers there. Verified the synced plugin.json on sync/external-plugins now has version 0.1.6 + the MCP server (node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/plugin-runtime/governed-brain.cjs). Catalog-on-main lands via the routine daily shared sync PR (300-file accumulation across all external plugins — not force-merged here, that's marketplace maintenance). Cowork remains installer-driven (build-cowork-zips skips MCP plugins).
